<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><b><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">PART I</font></u></b></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Forward Looking Statements and Risk Factors</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">This Form 10-K contains certain
forward-looking statements which are based on management&#146;s current
expectations.&#160; These forward-looking
statements are subject to certain risks and uncertainties. The words &#147;believe,&#148;
&#147;expect,&#148; &#147;anticipate,&#148; &#147;intend,&#148; &#147;estimate,&#148; &#147;will,&#148; &#147;should,&#148; &#147;could,&#148; and
other expressions that indicate future events and trends identify
forward-looking statements.&#160; The Company
has identified risk factors which could cause actual results to differ
substantially from the forward-looking statements.&#160; These risk factors include, but are not
limited to, general economic conditions, changes in interest rates,
availability of financing for both manufactured home buyers and suppliers, real
estate values, adverse weather conditions, the economic viability of our
customers and vendors, and availability of qualified employees.&#160; In addition, industry conditions that may
have an adverse impact on future results include, but are not limited to, low
barriers of entry, changes and/or enforcement in legislation or regulations, and
competitive pressure on both the purchasing of used axles and tires from
manufactured housing dealers and the selling of refurbished axles and tires to
manufactured housing factories.</font></i></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">All tabular dollar amounts are in thousands
and all period references are to the Company&#146;s fiscal period unless otherwise
indicated.</font></i></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">ITEM 1.&#160;
BUSINESS</font></u></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">T.J.T., Inc., an Idaho
corporation, was established in 1977. T.J.T., Inc. merged with and into T.J.T.,
Inc., a Washington corporation on December&nbsp;13, 1994.&#160; The Company&#146;s corporate office is located at
843 N. Washington Street, Emmett, Idaho 83617.&#160;
Information about the Company is available on the internet at
www.tjt-inc.com.&#160; Information contained
or referenced on our website is not incorporated by reference and does not form
a part of this Annual Report on Form 10-K.&#160;
Copies of our Annual Report on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form
10-Q, and the Executive Officers&#146; Code of Ethics are available free of charge
by calling 1-800-458-3555.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company has recycling and distribution locations in Emmett, Idaho;
Woodland, California; Chehalis, Washington; and Platteville, Colorado.&#160; The Company also manufactures hanger parts in
Eugene, Oregon which are used by the manufactured housing producers to attach
axles to homes. &#160;The Company maintained a
sales office in Salem, Oregon and a recycling and distribution plant in
Phoenix, Arizona until June&nbsp;of 2003 when both locations were closed.&#160; The Company operates in Arizona, New Mexico, Kansas,
and southern California through NewCo Tire and Axle, L.L.C. (NewCo), a joint
venture established in June&nbsp;2003.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company has two business lines: repairing
and reconditioning axles and tires for the manufactured housing industry, and
distribution of after-market accessory products to manufactured housing dealers
and set-up contractors, as well as siding to on-site house builders.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Recent Events</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The manufactured housing industry recovery that began in 2004 was preceded
by several years of decreases in manufactured housing production due to extensive
repossessions and decreased consumer demand resulting from tightened credit
standards.&#160; In 2004, production in the Company&#146;s
thirteen state market area increased by two percent from fiscal 2003 according
to statistics from the <i><font style="font-style:italic;">Institute for Building
Technology and Safety, Inc.,</font></i> a non-profit governmental organization
providing</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">2</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='2',FILE='C:\JMS\jpatrow\04-15333-1\task264925\15333-1-ba-01.htm',USER='jpatrow',CD='Dec 29 14:37 2004' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">independent evaluations, research, and technical assistance to federal
government agencies and formally known as <i><font style="font-style:italic;">National Conference of
States on Building Codes and Standards.</font></i></font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In January&nbsp;2004, the property lease for the Centralia, Washington
facility expired.&#160; Therefore, in December&nbsp;2003,
the Washington facility was moved to a new location in Chehalis, Washington.&#160; The Company entered into a lease with option
to purchase agreement in August&nbsp;2003.&#160;
The Company may execute the purchase option at any time prior to the
expiration of the lease.&#160; &#160;&#160;Leasehold improvements of approximately
$99,000 were made to the property.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In June&nbsp;2003, the Company entered into a joint venture, NewCo,
with West States Recycling, Inc.&#160; NewCo engages
</font>in the
manufactured housing axle and tire recycling business in the Arizona and
New Mexico, Kansas, and southern California market areas with plans to expand
into other market areas.&#160; The Company has
a 50 percent ownership interest, and under the terms of the joint venture, the
Company will receive 40 percent of&#160; net
profits or losses.&#160; In addition, the
Company leases trucks and equipment to the joint venture.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Axle and Tire Reconditioning</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company buys used axles and tires from manufactured housing dealers
throughout the western United States.&#160;
The axles and tires are detached from the manufactured homes when they are
placed on a pad or foundation upon sale and delivery of the manufactured home
by the dealer or third party.&#160; The
Company also buys used axles and tires from independent brokers.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In accordance with criteria set forth in The <i><font style="font-style:italic;">Federal
Manufactured Housing Construction and Safety Standards, Section&nbsp;3280.904(b)</font></i>,
more commonly known as &#147;The HUD Code,&#148; after each use the axles are dismantled,
cleaned, inspected, and rebuilt as necessary.&#160;
This is done in compliance with recycling guidelines and quality
assurance programs established and administered by independent third party
listing companies.&#160; Approximately 30
axles are rebuilt for each eight man-hour shift.&#160; Tires are graded and repaired.&#160; Axles and tires are then sold to manufactured
housing factories.&#160; Each axle and tire
assembly is used and recycled approximately three times a year.&#160; The tires and axles are sold principally to
manufactured housing factories and are delivered to their sites.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Sales of reconditioned axles and tires represented 74 percent, 77
percent, and 76 percent of total revenues for the years ended September&nbsp;30,
2004, 2003, and 2002, respectively.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Accessories and Siding Distribution</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company sells manufactured housing accessories such as vinyl
skirting, piers, and other ancillary products to manufactured housing dealers
and set-up contractors.&#160; The Company
sells vinyl siding to the site-built housing market.&#160; In 2004, the Company began selling metal
buildings under the accessories and siding distribution segment.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Sales of accessories and siding represented 26 percent, 23 percent, and
24 percent of total revenues for the years ended September&nbsp;30, 2004, 2003,
and 2002, respectively.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Regulatory Matters</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Department of Transportation (DOT) regulations govern the maximum per
tire load limit for tires used in the transportation of manufactured homes,
which in turn dictates the number of axles required for the transport of these
homes.&#160; Approximately seven axles are
required for transport of the average manufactured home.&#160; Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)
regulations require each axle to be reconditioned prior to every use by a
certified recycler, and further require periodic inspections of the recycling
facilities by an approved third party inspector.&#160; Future changes in DOT or</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='3',FILE='C:\JMS\jpatrow\04-15333-1\task264925\15333-1-ba-01.htm',USER='jpatrow',CD='Dec 29 14:37 2004' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">HUD rules and regulations may impact the demand for the Company&#146;s axle
and tire reconditioning services.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Target Market and Supply</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company&#146;s target market is the
manufactured housing industry and the site-built construction industry.&#160; The Company sells to manufactured housing
factories, manufactured housing dealers, set-up contractors, and site-built
contractors and remodelers.&#160; The Company&#146;s
major customers are manufactured housing factories.&#160; Three manufacturers collectively represented
more than 40 percent of total sales during 2004:</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<table border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" width="70%" style="border-collapse:collapse;margin-left:1.0in;width:70.0%;">
 <tr>
  <td width="76%" valign="top" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:76.94%;">
  <p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Oakwood/Clayton Homes
  Corporation</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="23%" valign="top" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:23.06%;">
  <p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">15 percent</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="76%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:76.94%;">
  <p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Fleetwood Enterprises,
  Inc.</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="23%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:23.06%;">
  <p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">15 percent</font></p>
  </td>

 <tr>
  <td width="76%" valign="top" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:76.94%;">

  </td>
  <td width="23%" valign="top" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:23.06%;">
  <p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">10 percent</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
</table>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company has no one vendor or broker that
supplies more than 10 percent of axle and tire purchases.&#160; It has one supplier of accessory products,
D-Mac, that supplies 11 percent of total accessory products purchased by the
Company.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Competition</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company operates in Idaho, Oregon, Washington, California,
Colorado, Utah, Nevada, Montana, Wyoming, Minnesota, Nebraska, North Dakota,
and South Dakota.&#160; The Company also
operates in Arizona, New Mexico, Kansas, and southern California through its
joint venture, NewCo Tire and Axle, L.L.C.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Axles and Tires</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In the Company&#146;s thirteen-state market area,
price competition is intense for both the purchase and sale of axles and tires.
The Company has two major competitors within its market area.&#160; Barriers to entry are low for the axle and
tire business, which could result in additional major competitors.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">An injunction was granted in January&nbsp;of
2002 prohibiting certain former employees and directors of the Company (the
Bradley Group) from competing against the Company&#146;s axle and tire business as
well as the housing accessories business.&#160;
The injunction expired on January&nbsp;1, 2004.&#160; Therefore, the Bradley Group is now competing
with the Company in the Oregon and Washington market areas.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company believes it has
greater financial resources, a larger geographical presence, and a better
reputation for quality, honesty, reliability, and service than its
competitors.&#160; The Company believes its
competitors have a lower cost structure that enables them to acquire market
share where price is the customers&#146; primary concern.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">All competitors are believed to be privately held companies having
between one and twenty-five employees.&#160;
The Company estimates that it has a 64 percent market share, and is
roughly two times larger than its largest competitor.&#160; Since the Company is the only company with
readily discernable revenues and industry totals are unknown, estimating market
share involves extrapolating numerous estimates and results in a market share
number subject to questionable accuracy.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company also competes in the Arizona, New Mexico, Kansas, and
southern California markets through a joint venture, NewCo Tire &amp; Axle,
L.L.C. (NewCo) which was formed in July&nbsp;of 2003.&#160; The Company believes there are two major
competitors in the joint venture&#146;s market area.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Accessories and Siding</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company competes for accessory, siding,
and metal building sales with building materials distributors and manufactured
housing wholesale suppliers which are numerous in all of the Company&#146;s market
areas.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company competes with numerous
competitors for accessories and siding sales with availability and price being
the principal methods of competition.&#160;
The Company believes it has lower prices than local stores providing
immediate access to accessories, and higher prices than regional distributors
that provide two to fourteen day access.&#160;
&#160;&#160;The Company also offers
engineered custom metal buildings while providing local customer service and
installation assistance.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Industry Overview</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Production of manufactured housing in the
Company&#146;s market area increased two percent from 2003 to 2004 after several
years of decline. &#160;Shipments of
manufactured homes increased three percent as well.&#160; The manufactured housing production and
shipment indicators were obtained from the <i><font style="font-style:italic;">Institute for Building
Technology and Safety, Inc., </font></i>formally known as the <i><font style="font-style:italic;">National Conference of States on Building Codes and Standards.</font></i>&#160;&#160;The Company expects to see market
conditions remain static in 2005 or to see slight increases similar to those
experienced in fiscal 2004.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h2 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Personnel</font></b></h2>

<h2 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h2>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company had a total of 101 employees as of September&nbsp;30, 2004.&#160; None of the Company&#146;s employees are members
of a union.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h5 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;text-decoration:underline;"><b><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">ITEM 2.&#160; PROPERTIES</font></u></b></h5>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The two properties owned by the Company are a warehouse in Emmett,
Idaho, and a processing facility in Platteville, Colorado.&#160; The Magna, Utah property, previously owned
and used as a small sales office by the Company, was sold in 2003.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company leases four properties located in Idaho, California,
Washington, and Oregon. &#160;Property located
in Emmett, Idaho is leased from two related parties, T.J.T. Enterprises and Sheldon-Homedale
Family, L.P.&#160; The property located in
Centralia, Washington was leased through January&nbsp;1, 2004.&#160; The recycling and distribution center located
in Centralia is now operated at a facility leased in Chehalis, Washington.&#160; The property previously leased for the Phoenix,
Arizona facility was subleased until the lease expired in January&nbsp;of 2004.&#160; The property leased and located in Eugene, Oregon
is only utilized for manufacturing hanger parts and sales of accessories.&#160; All properties are adequate and suitable for
the needs of the location and are being fully utilized.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">ITEM 3. LEGAL PROCEEDINGS</font></u></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company has no material
legal proceedings to report.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h5 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;text-decoration:underline;"><b><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">ITEM 4. SUBMISSION OF MATTERS TO A VOTE OF SECURITY HOLDERS</font></u></b></h5>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Shareholders were not asked to vote on any matters during the quarter
ended September&nbsp;30, 2004.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><b><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">EXECUTIVE OFFICERS OF THE
REGISTRANT</font></u></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The schedule&nbsp;below shows the names and
certain information regarding all of the executive officers of TJT as of September&nbsp;30,
2004.&#160; Each executive officer has a
one-year term of office.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">5</font></p>

<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<table border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" width="100%" style="border-collapse:collapse;width:100.0%;">
 <tr>
  <td width="24%" valign="bottom" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:24.76%;">

  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.12%;">
  <p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="12%" valign="bottom" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:12.94%;">
  <p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:8.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Age</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.48%;">
  <p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="57%" valign="bottom" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:57.7%;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 2.5in;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Terrence J. Sheldon</font></b>
- Mr. Sheldon is the founder and principal stockholder of the Company and has
served as President since October&nbsp;1986 and Chief Executive Officer since
1994.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Larry B. Prescott</font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> &#150; Mr. Prescott
has served as Senior Vice President, Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer
since January&nbsp;1999.&#160; Previously, he
served as Vice President and Portfolio Manager at U.S. Bancorp in
Portland.&#160; Mr. Prescott received a B.A.
in Business from Boise State University.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Cindy M. Truchot</font></b> &#150; Ms. Truchot is a CPA and has served as
Vice President and Controller since February&nbsp;2002.&#160; Previously, she served as Controller of a
privately owned technology based company as well as serving several years in the
public accounting sector.&#160; Ms. Truchot
received a B.S. in Business Administration, accounting emphasis, from Idaho
State University.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">John W. Eames III</font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> - Mr. Eames
has served the Company in various positions since 1991.&#160; He was appointed Corporate Secretary in August&nbsp;2001,
an Executive Officer in August&nbsp;2002, and a Vice President in February&nbsp;of
2004.&#160;&#160; Previously, Mr. Eames served as
National Sales Manager for a log home manufacturer in Idaho and held several
management positions with Boise Cascade Corporation&#146;s Housing Division.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><b><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">PART II</font></u></b></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">ITEM 5.&#160;

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company has never paid dividends to shareholders and does not
expect to pay dividends in the foreseeable future.&#160; The Company intends to use future earnings
for reinvestment in its business.&#160; The
Company is prohibited from paying cash dividends by the revolving line of
credit agreement with its principal lender.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Information relating to compensation plans under
which equity securities of the Company are authorized for issuance are set
forth under &#147;Equity Compensation Plan Information&#148; in the Company&#146;s definitive
Proxy Statement which is incorporated herein by reference.</font></p>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">ITEM 7. MANAGEMENT&#146;S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS
OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS</font></u></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">This Form 10-K contains certain
forward-looking statements which are based on management&#146;s current
expectations.&#160; The words &#147;believe,&#148; &#147;expect,&#148;
&#147;anticipate,&#148; &#147;intend,&#148; &#147;estimate,&#148; &#147;will,&#148; &#147;should,&#148; &#147;could,&#148; and other
expressions that indicate future events and trends identify forward-looking
statements. The forward-looking statements such as those made in &#147;Company Strategy&#148;
regarding growth for the housing accessories segment and new product
development, as well as comments made in &#147;Overview&#148; regarding expansion of
NewCo market areas are subject to certain risks and uncertainties.&#160; Additional risk factors identified by the
Company that could cause actual results to differ substantially from the
forward-looking statements include, but are not limited to, general economic
conditions, availability of financing for both manufactured home buyers and
suppliers, real estate values, adverse weather conditions, the economic
viability of our customers and vendors, and availability of qualified
employees.&#160; In addition, industry
conditions that may have an adverse impact on future results include, but are
not limited to, low barriers of entry, changes and/or enforcement in
legislation or regulations, and competitive pressure on both the purchasing of
used axles and tires from manufactured housing dealers and the selling of
refurbished axles and tires to manufactured housing factories. The Management&#146;s
Discussion and Analysis should be read in conjunction with the Financial
Statements and accompanying notes for the year ended September&nbsp;30,
2004.&#160; All period references are to the
Company&#146;s fiscal periods unless otherwise indicated.</font></i></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Overview</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">T.J.T., Inc. has two business lines: axle and tire reconditioning, and
accessories and siding distribution.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Axle and tire reconditioning sales represent approximately 74 percent
of consolidated sales.&#160; Axles and tires
are purchased from manufactured homes dealers and reconditioned in four
locations in the western United States.&#160;
After axles are reconditioned and certified and tires are inspected and
graded, they are sold primarily to manufactured housing factories.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Axle and tire reconditioning is performed at the Company&#146;s locations in
Emmett, Idaho; &#160;Chehalis, Washington;
Woodland, California; and Platteville, Colorado.&#160; The Company maintains a manufacturing
facility in Eugene, Oregon which manufactures metal hanger parts for attaching
axles to manufactured homes and frame components used on manufactured home
chassis, and sells dealer accessories items.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company sells accessories to manufactured
home dealers and set-up contractors from all its locations.&#160; The major product lines are vinyl skirting,
piers, and related products through the Company&#146;s distribution channels which
comprise approximately 650 dealers.&#160; The
Company also sells vinyl siding to the site-built construction industry out of
its Emmett location.&#160; In 2004 the Company
began selling metal buildings primarily to agricultural markets and registered the
trade name Anchor Metal Buildings.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company is a 50 percent owner in a joint
venture limited liability corporation, NewCo Tire &amp; Axle, L.L.C. (NewCo),
which operates an axle and tire reconditioning facility in Arizona and services
the Arizona, New Mexico, Kansas and parts of southern California market
areas.&#160; Plans for expansion into other
market areas are currently underway. &#160;&#160;The Company accounts for the joint venture
using</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">8</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='8',FILE='C:\JMS\jpatrow\04-15333-1\task264925\15333-1-ba-01.htm',USER='jpatrow',CD='Dec 29 14:37 2004' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the equity method and does not consolidate
the income as the investment does not meet the criteria for consolidation
outlined in the Financial Accounting Standards Board Interpretation No. 46, <i><font style="font-style:italic;">Consolidation of Variable Interest Entities &#150; an Interpretation of ARB
No. 51.</font></i> The Company recognized $224,000 of undistributed earnings
related to its investment in the joint venture, which contributed more than 20
percent to net income for the Company as a whole.&#160; Separate financial statements of NewCo will
be filed as an Exhibit in Item 15 of an amended Form 10-K within 90 days
following the joint venture&#146;s year end, which is October&nbsp;31, 2004.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Results of Operations</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">During 2004, the manufactured housing industry experienced an increase in
shipments of approximately three percent in the Company&#146;s market area according
to the <i><font style="font-style:italic;">Institute for Building Technology and Safety, Inc.,
</font></i>formally known as<i><font style="font-style:italic;"> National Conference of
States on Building Codes and Standards.</font></i>&#160;&#160; The Company&#146;s sales for the same period
reflect a decrease of only .1 percent.&#160; The
Company&#146;s ongoing operations increased its market share from 2003 to 2004 by 11
percent after excluding $1.9 million of sales generated in the first half of
2003 by the Company&#146;s Arizona facility.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The 2004 fiscal period reflects operating profit for each location
compared to losses in 2003 in Colorado and Arizona.&#160; The following table summarizes the Company&#146;s
revenues and expenses by major categories as a percent of sales for 2004, 2003
and 2002:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.4%;">

  </td>

  <td width="48%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:48.64%;">

  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.4%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>
  </td>

  </td>
  <td width="14%" valign="bottom" style="border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;border-left:none;border-right:none;border-top:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:14.34%;">
  <p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:8.0pt;font-weight:bold;">2003</font></b></p>

  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="border:none;border-top:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.4%;">

  <td width="14%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:14.44%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3,569</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.36%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="14%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:14.24%;">

  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.36%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Net Sales</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Net sales were virtually the same in 2004 as compared to 2003.&#160; In 2004, sales generated from the dealer
accessories and siding segment increased 15 percent from 2003 while sales from
the axles and tires segment declined five percent during the same period.&#160; However, the Company&#146;s Arizona facility,
which was closed mid-year in 2003, generated $1.9 million of sales to the axle
and tire segment in fiscal 2003.&#160; Axle
and tire sales generated by locations operating in both 2004 and 2003 increased
by eight percent.&#160; Therefore, sales
volumes of the Company&#146;s five remaining locations in 2004 largely offset the loss
of the 2003 Arizona sales.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Net sales during 2003 declined by three percent compared to the same
period in 2002 primarily due to the closure of the Arizona location during the
2003 fiscal year.&#160; Increases in sales of
axles and tires at the Platteville, Colorado facility partially offset the
decrease in Arizona sales.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Gross Margin</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company&#146;s gross profit margin increased three percent in 2004
compared to 2003.&#160; Gross margin was positively
impacted by higher sales prices for axles and tires, lower purchase costs of
used tires, and the elimination of lower margin sales generated by the Phoenix,
Arizona facility which was closed in July&nbsp;2003.&#160; In addition, higher margin sales of the accessories
and siding segment increased 15 percent in 2004 compared to 2003.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company&#146;s gross margin increased .4 percent in 2003 compared to
2002 as a result of higher selling prices of axles and tires in the final
quarter of 2003, which were offset by the lower margin sales at the Arizona
facility.&#160; The Arizona location operated
for the first nine months of 2003 but was in operation for the entire year of
2002.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Selling, General, and Administrative</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In 2004, selling, general and administrative expenses for the Company
increased by 10 percent compared to 2003.&#160;
The increase is primarily due to higher selling expenses associated with
expansion of the dealer accessories product line and performance based
incentive pay offset by elimination of overhead expense associated with the
Arizona location incurred during the first three quarters of 2003.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Selling, general and administrative expenses decreased by almost 10
percent in 2003 compared to 2002 primarily due to a significant reduction of
legal fees associated with prior year legal proceedings and a reduction of
overhead expenses as a result of closing the Arizona location in July&nbsp;of
2003.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Net Income</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Net income tripled in 2004 increasing to $397,000 compared to $132,000
in 2003.&#160; Higher margins generated from
an increase in higher margin accessories sales along with higher selling prices
and lower purchase costs in the axle and tire segment increased gross profit by
$573,000 in 2004 compared to 2003.&#160; All
locations open in 2004 operated at a profit.&#160;
The 2003 gross profit reflects losses of $98,000 related to the Phoenix,
Arizona facility.&#160; The increased margins
were offset by higher selling, general and administrative expenses resulting
from increased sales personnel related to expanding the dealer accessory and
after market business and incentive compensation for performance based bonus
plans.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company also recognized $224,000 of
undistributed income in 2004 and $63,000 in 2003 from its interest in NewCo.&#160;&#160; Rental income received for equipment leased
to the joint venture in 2004 was $26,000 compared to $16,000 received in the
four months during operation in 2003.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Net income for fiscal period 2003 was $132,000 compared to a loss of
$139,000 before the cumulative effect of implementing <i><font style="font-style:italic;">SFAS</font></i>
<i><font style="font-style:italic;">142, Goodwill and Other Intangible Assets</font></i>
in the first</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">10</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='10',FILE='C:\JMS\jpatrow\04-15333-1\task264925\15333-1-ba-01.htm',USER='jpatrow',CD='Dec 29 14:37 2004' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">quarter of 2002.&#160; The improvement
was primarily due to a decrease of selling, general and administrative expenses
of $450,000 and improved operating results at the Company&#146;s Colorado and
California locations.&#160;&#160; Losses generated
by the Arizona facility during the 2003 and 2002 fiscal periods were $98,000
and $339,000, respectively.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Income recognized on investment property sales was $170,000, $68,000,
and $116,000 in 2004, 2003, and 2002, respectively.&#160; The Company plans to liquidate the remaining
investment properties, which have a book value of $224,000 as of September&nbsp;30,
2004, as permitted by market conditions.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h2 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Seasonality</font></i></b></h2>

<h2 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h2>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The manufactured housing
industry and the site-built construction industry are seasonal within the
majority of the Company&#146;s market area.&#160;
Typically, sales for the months from November&nbsp;through March&nbsp;are
lower than for other months due to weather and ground conditions.&#160; Assuming normal weather conditions, the
Company expects the quarters ended September&nbsp;30 and June&nbsp;30 to be
higher volume quarters and the quarter ended March&nbsp;31 to be the lowest
volume quarter.&#160;&#160; The following table
summarizes operating results by quarter and demonstrates the seasonal nature of
TJT&#146;s operations:</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:1.0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<table border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" width="100%" style="border-collapse:collapse;width:100.0%;">
 <tr>
  <td width="43%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:43.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="12%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:12.0%;">
  <p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:8.0pt;font-weight:bold;">December&nbsp;31</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="12%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:12.0%;">
  <p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:8.0pt;font-weight:bold;">March&nbsp;31</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="12%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:12.0%;">
  <p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:8.0pt;font-weight:bold;">June&nbsp;30</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.0%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>
  </td>
  <td width="12%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:12.0%;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h2 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Liquidity and Capital Resources</font></b></h2>

<h2 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h2>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Operating
activities</font></i> for 2004 used cash of $218,000 compared to
operating activities in 2003 which provided cash of $306,000. &#160;In 2004, the Company increased housing accessories
inventory levels as a result of adding new product lines and changing suppliers
of certain tie down accessories.&#160; In
addition, a contractual agreement with a major customer who also supplies tires
and axles to the Company, significantly reduced the payment terms on purchases
made by the Company.&#160; &#160;Prepaid expenses and other current assets
increased by $95,000 in 2004, of which $87,000 is a result of direct deposit
amounts related to the October&nbsp;1<sup>st</sup> payroll clearing the bank in
September, as well as increases in advances to tire and axle buyers.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Investing activities</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> used $11,000
of cash during 2004 compared to $1,000 in 2003.&#160;
Amounts received on notes receivable and investment property sales during
2004 were more than offset by $122,000 of leasehold improvements made to the
Chehalis, Washington and Eugene, Oregon facilities along with $105,000 invested
in trucks, trailers, and equipment.&#160; The
Company invested approximately $40,000 in necessary upgrades to the information
systems and accounting software during 2004.&#160;
&#160;&#160;Also, $17,000 was used to further
develop property parcels owned by the Company to increase market appeal.&#160;&#160; Investment property sales contributed $158,000
and $153,000 to cash flow from investing activities for the periods ending September&nbsp;30,
2004 and 2003, respectively.&#160; The Company
will continue to focus on liquidating its current investment property holdings
and expects to see similar</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">11</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">results in fiscal 2005.&#160;&#160; The Company made an advance in exchange for
a note receivable from a related party which is discussed further in Note K of
the financial statements.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">On March&nbsp;31, 2004, the Company renewed a
$350,000 revolving credit facility that matures on March&nbsp;31, 2005 and is
secured by receivables and inventory.&#160;
The interest rate on the credit line is the prime rate plus one
percent.&#160; As of September&nbsp;30, 2004,
the Company has not borrowed on the line and is in compliance with all
covenants.&#160;&#160; The Company expects that
cash flow from operations combined with the line of credit will be a sufficient
source of liquidity to fund operations.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Critical Accounting Policies</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company&#146;s financial statements have been
prepared in conformity with generally accepted accounting principles.&#160; In preparing the financial statements,
management makes many estimates and assumptions that affect the amounts and
disclosures reported in the financial statements and related accompanying
notes.&#160; Changes in most of the estimates
and assumptions would not likely materially affect the Company&#146;s financial
position and results of operations.&#160;
However, calculations related to inventory, shipping expenses and
deferred tax assets involve subjective judgments, estimates, and assumptions
that could produce materially different results if different judgments,
estimates, or assumptions were used.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Inventory</font></i></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Inventory is stated at lower of cost or
market. Cost of the Company&#146;s tire and axle inventory includes unprocessed or
raw tires and axles as well as processed or finished tires and axles.&#160; A portion of the costs incurred in acquiring
and processing the tires and axles is allocated to finished inventory based on
the number of tires and axles processed at the end of the period.&#160; The Company estimates per unit processing and
gathering costs based upon labor and manufacturing overhead costs.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company reviews inventory for slow moving
or obsolete items on a regular basis.&#160;
Inventory is written-down based on specific identification, taking into
consideration demand in certain markets and other industry conditions.&#160; If actual demand or market conditions are
less favorable than those estimated, additional write-downs may be necessary.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Shipping Allocation</font></i></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company allocates to cost of goods sold a
portion of the costs associated with gathering and distributing products
(shipping costs).&#160; These expenses include
labor for drivers, fuel, equipment operating leases as well as maintenance and
depreciation, along with other expenses related to travel and per diem.&#160;&#160; These shipping costs are allocated based
upon an estimate of the level of gathering and distribution utilized by
department and segment of business for each facility operated by the Company. &#160;The estimates associated with gathering and
delivering tires and axles to manufactured housing factories are allocated to
cost of goods sold for each location.&#160;
Although the estimates used to allocate shipping costs are subjective,
they do not change the net income results.&#160;
However, the allocation to cost of goods sold could impact the gross
margin analysis for both business segments from period to period if the
estimated levels of shipping costs change significantly.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Income Taxes</font></i></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company&#146;s accompanying Balance Sheets
include deferred tax assets resulting from temporary differences, which are
expected to reduce future taxable income.&#160;
Management estimates the realizability of these assets based upon
forecasted taxable income.&#160; The
realizability of these assets is reassessed at the end of each reporting period
or as significant events occur which may impact management&#146;s assessment.&#160; Failure to achieve forecasted taxable income
could affect the realization of certain deferred tax assets, and could result
in the recording of a valuation reserve.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">12</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:11.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='12',FILE='C:\JMS\jpatrow\04-15333-1\task264925\15333-1-ba-01.htm',USER='jpatrow',CD='Dec 29 14:37 2004' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Off Balance Sheet
Arrangements</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Joint Venture</font></i></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company entered into a joint venture,
NewCo, to engage in the manufactured housing axle and tire recycling business
in Arizona, New Mexico, Kansas and southern California.&#160; The Company has 50 percent ownership interest
in the joint venture and will receive 40 percent of the net profits or losses.&#160; The Company also receives rental income from
NewCo for equipment leased to the joint venture.&#160;&#160; The Company recognized $224,000 and $63,000
of income in 2004 and 2003, respectively, related to the investment as is
further described in Footnote N of the financial statements.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company contributed $130,000 in cash and
$259,000 of inventory during 2003.&#160; No capital
contributions were made during 2004.&#160;
Further capital contributions to NewCo are limited to an additional $20,000
in aggregate unless both parties agree to the additional contributions and are
willing to contribute 50 percent of the required capital.&#160; The Company&#146;s exposure is limited to amounts
contributed as of the end of 2003 as well as amounts included in trade
receivables, which approximate $2,000 in 2004 and $16,000 in 2003.&#160; The Company accounts for its interest in the
joint venture using the equity method.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Contractual Obligations</font></i></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The following table
summarizes the Company&#146;s significant contractual obligations at September&nbsp;30,
2004, and the effect the obligations are expected to have on the Company&#146;s cash
flow in future periods.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:2.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
