  <td width="252" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="13" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="8" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="81" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="13" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="9" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="82" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="13" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="9" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="70" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="13" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="10" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="75" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="13" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="10" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="70" style="border:none;"></td>
  <td width="5" style="border:none;"></td>
 </tr>
</table>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The timing of payment
amounts of the obligations listed above are estimated based upon current
information.&#160;&#160; The timing of payments and
actual amounts paid may be different depending on potential changes to lease
agreements.&#160; The amounts above include
leases that are non-cancelable.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company does not have
any long-term debt obligations or any other agreements that require fixed or
minimum quantities to be purchased that are enforceable or legally binding
purchase agreements.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Company Strategy</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company will endeavor to maintain its market share and profit
margins in the axle and tire segment of the business.&#160; The Company expects demand to increase over
the next year and as a result could encounter supply shortages in used tires
and axles.&#160; The Company plans to protect
profit margins through careful planning, good purchasing practices, and
judicious price increases for reconditioned axles and used tires.&#160; Prior experience indicates that should the
demand for used tires and axles exceed availability, it would be possible to
supplement supply with new product at comparable profit margins.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company&#146;s growth strategies for fiscal 2005 include research and
development of carefully selected new product lines for addition into the
accessories and siding distribution segment, and a significantly expanded
marketing effort for Anchor Metal Buildings&#174;.&#160;
The marketing plan for Anchor Metal Buildings&#174; includes strategic
placement of model building sales centers, providing one-stop shopping service
to customers throughout the northwest and northern California, as well as
expanded product offerings through improved design and engineering.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">13</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">These strategies are consistent with our continuing quest to develop a
more diversified and equal business base for the Company.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">ITEM 7A. QUANTITATIVE AND QUALITATIVE
DISCLOSURE ABOUT MARKET RISK</font></u></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company is not required to provide this information pursuant to
Item 305(e) of Regulation S-K.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">T.J.T.,
INC.</font></b></p>

<h4 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><a name="NotesToFinancialStatements"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">NOTES TO FINANCIAL
STATEMENTS</font></b></a></h4>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">September&nbsp;30, 2004, 2003
and 2002</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<table border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" width="100%" style="border-collapse:collapse;width:100.0%;">
 <tr>
  <td width="100%" valign="top" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:100.0%;">
  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">NOTE A - SIGNIFICANT ACCOUNTING POLICIES</font></b></h3>
  <p style="line-height:50%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:50%;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="100%" valign="top" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 3.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:100.0%;">
  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;line-height:20%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:20%;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>
  </td>
 </tr>
</table>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Business Activity</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company is engaged in the business of
repairing and reconditioning axles and tires for the manufactured housing industry.&#160; The Company also sells skirting and other
aftermarket accessories to manufactured housing dealers, and vinyl siding
primarily to the site-built housing market.&#160;
The Company grants trade credit to customers in Idaho, Oregon,
California, Utah, Washington, Montana, Colorado, Wyoming, Arizona, Minnesota, Nebraska,
Nevada, North Dakota, and South Dakota, substantially all of whom are
manufactured housing factories, manufactured housing dealers, site-built home
contractors or siding contractors.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company operates in two business
segments:&#160; Axle and Tire Reconditioning
and Accessories and Siding Distribution.&#160;
These segments have been determined by evaluating the Company&#146;s internal
reporting structure and nature of products offered.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Major Suppliers and Customers</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company had no single supplier of axles
and tires that represented 10 percent or more of total purchases.&#160; The Company had one supplier of accessory
products, D-Mac, that supplied 11 percent of total accessories purchases. The
Company has certain major customers for reconditioned axles and tires, all of
which are manufactured housing producers.&#160;
Three companies have purchases representing 10 percent or more of sales:</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:2.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.32%;">

 <tr>
  <td width="50%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:50.3%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-autospace:none;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Company B</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.3%;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Cash and Cash Equivalents</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company considers all
highly liquid investments purchased with a maturity of three months or less to
be cash equivalents.&#160; Cash balances are
invested in highly rated financial institutions and may at times exceed FDIC
insured limits.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Receivables</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company performs credit history checks and limited financial
analysis before credit terms are offered to customers.&#160; Accounts receivable are generally
unsecured.&#160; Bad debts are accounted for
using the allowance method.&#160; Expense is
recognized based on an estimate of uncollectible accounts.&#160; Trade receivables are reported in the balance
sheet net of any chargeoffs and the allowance for doubtful accounts.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Notes receivable consist
primarily of amounts owed by individuals as a result of the sale of real estate
and are generally secured by the real estate sold.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Property, Plant and Equipment</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Property, plant and
equipment is stated at cost.&#160;
Depreciation is calculated using the straight-line method over the estimated
useful lives for financial reporting purposes.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">19</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:11.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

</div>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Useful lives for property,
plant and equipment are as follows:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Repairs and maintenance costs which do not extend the estimated useful
lives of property, plant and equipment are charged to expense in the period
incurred.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Deferred Charges and Other Assets</font></b></h3>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Deferred charges and other
assets consist primarily of contractual rights under a split-dollar life
insurance agreement on the life of Terrence Sheldon.&#160; The Company owns the policy and pays the
premium, and will recover all premiums paid by the Company in 2018, or upon the
death of Mr. Sheldon, if earlier.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Goodwill</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Goodwill previously consisted of the excess of purchase price paid over
net assets acquired. Effective October&nbsp;1, 2001, the Company implemented
SFAS 142, and performed the initial goodwill test.&#160; The impairment test involved estimating fair
market values for tangible assets and liabilities and comparing the net
tangible values to quoted market prices for the Company&#146;s stock.&#160; As of October&nbsp;1, 2001 the Company&#146;s net
tangible value was substantially in excess of quoted market prices for the
Company&#146;s stock and a cumulative effect of accounting change of $748,000 was
recorded.&#160; The cumulative effect of
accounting change consists of a $790,000 writedown of goodwill assigned to the
Company&#146;s axle and tire reconditioning segment, offset by a $42,000 tax
benefit.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

  <td width="84%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:84.98%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-autospace:none;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Effect of
  accounting change (pre-tax)</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.0%;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Stock Based Compensation</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
Company has a stock option plan which allows officers, directors and key
employees to receive non-qualified and incentive stock options. &#160;&#160;The Company awarded 20,000 stock options to
directors during the quarter ended March&nbsp;31, 2004 with exercise prices
ranging between $.70 and $.80.&#160; During
the quarter ended June&nbsp;30, 2004, the Company granted 5,000 stock options
with an exercise price of $.70 to a director.&#160;
There were no options granted during the first and final quarters of
fiscal 2004.&#160; All options granted will
become vested at a rate of 20 percent each year for a period of five years from
the grant date.&#160; As of September&nbsp;30,
2004, 380,000 shares of stock were available for future option grants.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As
of October&nbsp;1, 2003, the Company adopted the fair value method of
accounting for stock options contained in Statement of Financial Standards (&#147;SFAS&#148;)
No. 123, <i><font style="font-style:italic;">Accounting for Stock-Based Compensation.</font></i>&#160; During the transition period, the Company
will be utilizing the prospective method under SFAS No. 148 <i><font style="font-style:italic;">Accounting for Stock-Based Compensation &#150; Transition and
Disclosures.&#160; </font></i>Stock options
granted subsequent to October&nbsp;1, 2003 will be expensed over the stock
option vesting period based on fair value which will be determined using the
Black-Scholes option-pricing method at the</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">20</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='20',FILE='C:\JMS\jpatrow\04-15333-1\task264925\15333-1-ba-01.htm',USER='jpatrow',CD='Dec 29 14:37 2004' -->



<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">

<div>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">date
the options are granted.&#160; Stock-based
compensation for the period ended September&nbsp;30, 2004 was $3,000.&#160; The net of tax impact on the financial
statements during fiscal 2004 approximated $2,000.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Deferred Gains</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Deferred gains include gains
on the sale of land held for investment where the Company provided virtually
100 percent financing to the buyer under an installment sale.&#160; The Company recognizes interest income and
then a reduction of the principal balance to the extent of payments received on
the related notes receivable until it has received 25 percent or more of the
original principal balance, at which point the remaining deferred gain is
recognized if the balance is deemed collectible.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Income Taxes</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Income taxes are accounted
for using the asset and liability method under which deferred income taxes are
determined based on differences between the financial reporting and tax basis
of assets and liabilities.&#160; Deferred
income taxes are measured by applying enacted tax rates and laws to taxable
years in which such differences are expected to reverse<b><font style="font-weight:bold;">.&#160;&#160; </font></b>Management has determined that
expected future earnings will be sufficient for all deferred tax assets to be
realizable before their expiration.&#160;
Accordingly, no valuation allowance has been offset against deferred tax
assets.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Earnings Per Share</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Basic earnings per share (EPS) is computed based on net income and the
weighted average number of common shares outstanding.&#160; Diluted EPS is similar to basic EPS except
that the weighted average of common shares outstanding is increased to include
the number of potential additional common shares that would have been
outstanding if the dilutive options had been exercised.&#160;&#160; Although basic EPS was not impacted by the
dilutive options for each year presented, 130,000, 123,000, and 88,000 of the
total options outstanding at September&nbsp;30, 2004, 2003, and 2002,
respectively, were not included in the diluted earnings per share calculation
because the exercise price of the options were greater than the average market
price of the common shares.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">21</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Revenue Recognition</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company recognizes revenue at the time delivery is made to the
customer, or when used by the customer in the case of consigned inventory.&#160; No agreements exist requiring any performance
by the Company subsequent to the sale.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Concentration of Credit Risk</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">All trade receivables are due from entities
involved in the housing industry and are unsecured.&#160; The accounting loss incurred if all parties
failed entirely to perform on their obligation is equal to the balance
outstanding for trade accounts receivable.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Notes receivable related to
sales of real estate held for investment are secured by real estate located in
Idaho and Utah.&#160; Other portions of the
notes receivable are secured by receivables. The accounting loss incurred if
all parties failed entirely to perform on their obligation is equal to the
balance outstanding on the notes receivable less amounts realizable from the
foreclosure and resale of the assets securing the notes receivable.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Fair Value of Financial Instruments</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company has a number of nonderivative
financial instruments, none of which are held for trading purposes.&#160; The recorded amounts of cash and cash
equivalents, accounts receivable, short-term borrowings, accounts payable and
accrued liabilities as presented in the financial statements approximate fair
value because of the short-term nature of these instruments.&#160; The recorded amount of short and long-term borrowings
approximates fair value as the interest rates approximate current competitive
rates.&#160; The estimated fair values have
been determined by the Company using available market information and
appropriate valuation methodologies.&#160;
Judgment is required in interpreting market data to develop the
estimates of fair value and the estimates are not necessarily indicative of
amounts the Company could realize in a current market exchange.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Significant Estimates</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The presentation of
financial statements in conformity with the accounting principles generally
accepted in the United States of America requires management to make estimates
and assumptions that affect the reported amounts of assets and liabilities and
the disclosure of contingent assets and liabilities at the date of the
financial statements, as well as the reported amounts of revenue and expenses
during the reporting period.&#160; Listed
below are the estimates and assumptions that management considers to be
significant in the preparation of its financial statements.&#160; Significant estimates used in preparing these
financial statements are as follows:</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Allowance
for Doubtful Accounts</font></u><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> &#151; The Company estimates losses for
uncollectible accounts based on the aging of the accounts receivable and an
evaluation of the likelihood of success in collecting the receivable.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Inventory
Lower-of-Cost-or-Market</font></u><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> &#150; The Company assesses the estimated net
realizable value of inventory on hand for the purposes of costing at the lower
of cost or market.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Inventory
Obsolescence Allowance</font></u><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> &#150; The Company evaluates the expected
salability and usefulness of inventory on hand for purposes of determining the
presence of obsolescence for old, defective, or outdated inventory.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .5in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">It is reasonably possible
that the significant estimates may change within the next year.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">22</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:11.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

</div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Reclassifications</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Certain 2003 and 2002
amounts have been reclassified to conform with 2004 presentation.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Recently Issued Accounting
Standards</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In
September&nbsp;2004, the Emerging Issues Task Force (EITF) reached a consensus
on EITF Issue No.&nbsp;04-10, <i><font style="font-style:italic;">Applying
Paragraph&nbsp;19 of FAS&nbsp;131 in Determining Whether to Aggregate Operating
Segments That Do Not Meet the Quantitative Thresholds.</font></i> The consensus
states that operating segments that do not meet the quantitative thresholds can
be aggregated only if aggregation is consistent with the objective and basic
principles of SFAS No.&nbsp;131, <i><font style="font-style:italic;">Disclosures
about Segments of an Enterprise and Related Information</font></i>, the
segments have similar economic characteristics, and the segments share a majority
of the aggregation criteria (a)-(e) listed in paragraph&nbsp;17 of
SFAS&nbsp;131. The effective date of the consensus in this Issue is for fiscal
years ending after October&nbsp;13, 2004.&#160;
If the Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB) ratifies EITF Issue
No. 04-10, the Company does not anticipate a material impact on the financial
statements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In March&nbsp;2004, the EITF reached a consensus on
the remaining portions of EITF 03-01, <i><font style="font-style:italic;">The
Meaning of Other-Than-Temporary Impairment and Its Application to Certain
Investments</font></i> with an effective date of June&nbsp;15, 2004. EITF 03-01
provides new disclosure requirements for other-than-temporary impairments on
debt and equity investments. Investors are required to disclose quantitative
information about: (i)&nbsp;the aggregate amount of unrealized losses, and
(ii)&nbsp;the aggregate related fair values of investments with unrealized
losses, segregated into time periods during which the investment has been in an
unrealized loss position of less than 12&nbsp;months and greater than 12&nbsp;months.
In addition, investors are required to disclose the qualitative information
that supports their conclusion that the impairments noted in the qualitative
disclosure are not other-than temporary. The Company does not expect EITF 03-01
to have a material impact on the financial statements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In March&nbsp;2004, the EITF reached a consensus on
EITF Issue No.&nbsp;03-6, <i><font style="font-style:italic;">Participating
Securities and the Two-Class Method under FASB Statement No.&nbsp;128, Earnings
per Share</font></i>. EITF Issue No.&nbsp;03-6 clarifies what constitutes a
participating security and provides further guidance in applying the two-class
method of calculating earnings per share. The consensuses reached by the Task
Force on EITF Issue No.&nbsp;03-6 were ratified by the FASB on March&nbsp;31,
2004, and are effective for reporting periods beginning after March&nbsp;31,
2004.&#160; The Company currently has only one
class of stock issued and does not expect EITF Issue No. 03-6 to materially
impact the financial statements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The FASB issued Statement of Financial Accounting Standards (SFAS) No.
148 in December&nbsp;2002.&#160; This
Statement amends FASB Statement No. 123, <i><font style="font-style:italic;">Accounting for Stock-Based
Compensation,</font></i> to provide alternative methods of transition for a
voluntary change to the fair value based method of accounting for stock-based
employee compensation.&nbsp; In addition, this Statement amends the disclosure
requirements of Statement 123 to require prominent disclosures in both annual
and interim financial statements about the method of accounting for stock-based
employee compensation and the effect of the method used on reported
results.&#160; Prior to fiscal 2004, the
Company accounted for its employee stock-based compensation using the intrinsic
value method while meeting the disclosure requirements of Statement No. 148.&#160;&#160;&#160; Effective October&nbsp;1, 2003, the Company
adopted the fair value method of accounting for stock options in accordance
with Statement No. 148.&#160; During the
transition period, the Company will utilize the prospective method.&#160; Stock options granted subsequent to October&nbsp;1,
2003 will be expensed over the stock options vesting period based on fair value
which will be determined using the Black-Scholes option-pricing method at the
date the options are granted.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">On March&nbsp;31, 2004, the FASB issued <i><font style="font-style:italic;">Revised Statement of Financial Accounting Standards-Share-Based Payment</font></i>,
effective for fiscal periods beginning after December&nbsp;15, 2004. This
Statement was published by the FASB on December&nbsp;16, 2004 and supercedes
SFAS 123, which allowed for footnote</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">23</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">disclosure of this expense.&#160; The
Company adopted Statement No. 148 in 2004 and, therefore, does not expect this
Statement to have a material impact on the financial statements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In
January&nbsp;2003, the FASB issued Interpretation No. 46, <i><font style="font-style:italic;">Consolidation
of Variable Interest Entities &#150; an interpretation of ARB No. 51</font></i>,
which provides guidance on the identification of and reporting for variable
interest entities.&#160; Interpretation No. 46
expands the criteria for consideration in determining whether a variable
interest entity should be consolidated.&#160;
Interpretation No. 46 is effective immediately for variable interest
entities created after January&nbsp;31, 2003, and to variable interest entities
in which an enterprise obtains an interest after that date.&#160; Interpretation No. 46 is effective for the
Company in 2003 because the Company entered into a joint venture with West
States Recycling, Inc. on June&nbsp;9, 2003.&#160;
The Company has a 50 percent interest in ownership and a 40 percent
interest in future net profits or losses.&#160;
The Interpretation requires unconsolidated variable interest entities to
be consolidated by their primary beneficiaries.&#160;
The primary beneficiary is the party that assumes the majority of the
risk which includes, but is not limited to, the entity&#146;s expected losses.&#160; Therefore, in accordance with Interpretation
No. 46, the Company will not consolidate the joint venture financial
information since the primary beneficiary is West States Recycling, Inc.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

  <p style="line-height:50%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:50%;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Inventories are stated at
the lower of cost (first-in, first-out and average cost methods) or market.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Depreciation expense was $170,000, $226,000, and $316,000 for 2004,
2003, and 2002, respectively.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<table border="1" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" width="100%" style="border:none;border-collapse:collapse;width:100.0%;">
 <tr>
  <td width="100%" valign="top" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:100.0%;">
  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">NOTE D &#150; NOTES RECEIVABLE</font></b></h3>
  <p style="line-height:50%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:50%;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="100%" valign="top" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 3.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:100.0%;">
  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;line-height:20%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:20%;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>
  </td>
 </tr>
</table>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Notes that management has
the intent and ability to hold for the foreseeable future or until maturity or
payoff are reported in the balance sheet at outstanding principal adjusted for
any related deferred income.&#160; Notes
receivable consist primarily of amounts owed by individuals resulting from the
sale of real estate and are generally secured by the real estate sold.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company made an advance
to a related party in exchange for a note receivable which is secured by real
estate.&#160; Current and long-term portion of
notes receivable reported in the accompanying</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">24</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:11.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">financial statements
includes amounts due from related parties totaling $94,000 and $120,000 as of September&nbsp;30,
2004 and 2003, respectively (See Note K).&#160;
Notes generally require minimum principal and interest repayments
monthly, quarterly or annually, with any remaining amounts due upon maturity or
resale of the underlying property.&#160;
Interest accrues at rates ranging between eight and twelve percent per
annum.&#160; Minimum principal repayments to
be received in future years ending September&nbsp;30 are as follows:</font></p>

  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">NOTE E - LEASES</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company leases vehicles,
administrative office space, manufacturing facilities, building and warehouse
space, and storage yard space.&#160; The leases,
which expire between December&nbsp;2004 and July&nbsp;2012, are classified as
operating leases.&#160; The leases have been
entered into with related parties and unaffiliated entities.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company entered into a
five year lease agreement for the Chehalis, Washington facility in 2003.&#160; The Company has the option to buy the
property at any time during the lease period or upon expiration of the
lease.&#160; There are no other significant
renewals or escalation clauses.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The future minimum payments
by fiscal year under non-cancelable operating lease agreements at September&nbsp;30,
2004 were:</font></p>

  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">624</font></p>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Mr. Mori, a Director of the
Company, owned property leased by the Company until May of 2002.&#160; The Company now leases the property from a
non-related party.&#160; T.J.T. Enterprises is
a partnership consisting of Terrence Sheldon, President and Chief Executive
Officer of the Company, and Jerry L. Radandt, a former officer of the
Company.&#160; Mr. Sheldon and Mr. Radandt are
equal partners in T.J.T. Enterprises.&#160;
Sheldon-Homedale Family, L.P. (Homedale) is a partnership owned by the
Sheldon family.&#160; Terrence Sheldon owns
five percent and is a general partner of Homedale.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<table border="1" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" width="100%" style="border:none;border-collapse:collapse;width:100.0%;">
 <tr>
  <td width="100%" valign="top" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:100.0%;">
  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">NOTE F - CREDIT FACILITY</font></b></h3>
  <p style="line-height:50%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:50%;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="100%" valign="top" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 3.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:100.0%;">
  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;line-height:20%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:20%;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>
  </td>
 </tr>
</table>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">On March&nbsp;31, 2004, the Company renewed a
$350,000 revolving credit facility that matures on March&nbsp;31, 2005 and is
secured by receivables and inventory.&#160;
The interest rate on the credit line is the prime rate plus one
percent.&#160; As of September&nbsp;30, 2004,
the Company has not borrowed on the line and is in compliance with all
covenants.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<table border="1" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" width="100%" style="border:none;border-collapse:collapse;width:100.0%;">
 <tr>
  <td width="100%" valign="top" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:100.0%;">
  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">NOTE G &#150;
  CUMULATIVE EFFECT OF ACCOUNTING CHANGE</font></b></h3>
  <p style="line-height:50%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:50%;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="100%" valign="top" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 3.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:100.0%;">
  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;line-height:20%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:20%;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>
  </td>
 </tr>
</table>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company implemented SFAS 142, <i><font style="font-style:italic;">Goodwill and Other Intangible Assets</font></i>, and performed the
initial goodwill impairment test in 2002.&#160;
The impairment test involved estimating fair market values for tangible
assets and liabilities and comparing the net tangible values to quoted market
prices for the Company&#146;s stock.&#160; As of October&nbsp;1,
2001 the Company&#146;s net tangible value was substantially in excess of quoted
market prices for the Company&#146;s stock and a cumulative effect of accounting
change of $748,000 was recorded.&#160; The
cumulative effect of accounting change consists of a $790,000 writedown of
goodwill assigned to the Company&#146;s axle and tire reconditioning segment, offset
by a $42,000 tax benefit.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Net income adjusted to exclude amortization
expense and related tax benefit is as follows:</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Authorized stock of the
Company consists of 10,000,000 shares of $.001 par value common stock and
5,000,000 shares of $.001 par value preferred stock.&#160; No shares of preferred stock have been
issued.</font></p>

  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">NOTE I &#150; STOCK OPTIONS</font></b></h3>
  <p style="line-height:50%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:50%;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company has two stock option plans which
allow key employees and directors of the Company to receive incentive and
non-qualified stock options.&#160; The
exercise price, term and other conditions applicable to each option granted
under the Company&#146;s plans are generally determined by the Compensation
Committee of the Board of Directors at the time of the grant of each option and
may vary with each option granted.&#160; No options
may have a term longer than ten years.&#160;
The plans allow up to 400,000 incentive, or nonqualified options to be
issued to key employees and up to 200,000 nonqualified options to be issued to
directors.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Option activity under the Company&#146;s stock
option plans is summarized as follows:</font></p>

<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">NOTE&nbsp; J - INCOME TAXES</font></b></h3>
  <p style="line-height:50%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:50%;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>

  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;line-height:20%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:20%;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company accounts for
income taxes as prescribed by Statement of Financial Accounting Standards No.
109, <i><font style="font-style:italic;">Accounting for Income Taxes,</font></i> which
requires deferred income taxes to be accounted for using the liability method
and allows recognition of operating loss and tax credit carryforwards as deferred
tax assets.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The components of income tax benefit (expense) from continuing
operations for the years ended September&nbsp;30 are as follows:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The net operating loss carry forward at September&nbsp;30, 2004 for
federal tax purpose of approximately $38,000 and state tax purposes of
approximately $</font>907,000
begins to expire in the years 2020 and 2006, respectively.</p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">28</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:11.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In October&nbsp;1996,
the Company made a personal loan to Jerry L. Radandt for $46,594 at an interest
rate of 12.22 percent.&#160;&#160; During fiscal
2001 the loan was amended to an interest rate of prime plus two percent and
secured by property, which was subsequently sold resulting in $30,000 of the
loan being paid off.&#160; The outstanding
principal loan balance, which is included in the totals specified above, is
$3,187 and $9,992 for September&nbsp;30, 2004 and 2003, respectively.&#160; Terrence Sheldon, President and Chief
Executive Officer of the Company, and Jerry L. Radandt are equal partners in
T.J.T. Enterprises.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In March&nbsp;2004, the Company made a loan
to Leo Radandt for $35,000 at an interest rate of 10 percent.&#160; A second loan was made in March&nbsp;2004 for
$10,000 at an interest rate of 10 percent for the first month and five percent
for every month thereafter.&#160; In August&nbsp;2004,
a third loan was made to Leo Radandt for $41,850 at an interest rate of 10
percent.&#160; These three notes are secured
by property with a fair market value of approximately $110,000 and will mature
in March&nbsp;2005.&#160; A fourth note was
made in August&nbsp;2004 for $6,990 at an interest rate of eight percent and is
secured by personal property and will mature in August&nbsp;2005. The
outstanding principal loan balance for all four notes on September&nbsp;30,
2004 was $86,042.&#160; Leo Radandt is the son
of Jerry L. Radandt, who is an equal partner with Terrence Sheldon, the
President and Chief Executive Office of the Company, in T.J.T.
Enterprises.&#160; In addition, Leo Radandt is
an outside supplier of tires and axles to the Company.&#160; The Company purchased approximately $866,000
of materials from Wholesale Tire and Axle, which is owned by Leo Radandt,
during fiscal 2004.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As more fully described in Note N, the
Company is a 50 percent partner in a joint venture, NewCo Tire &amp; Axle,
L.L.C., with West States Recycling, Inc.&#160;
</font>The Company
has a 40 percent interest in net profits and losses.&#160; In addition, the Company leases trucks and
equipment to the joint venture.&#160; Rental
income recognized from NewCo during 2004 and 2003 was $26,000 and $16,000,
respectively.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company purchases piers
and other materials used to set-up manufactured homes from D-Mac, LLC, which is
owned by two individuals with each individual owning 50 percent. Robert B.
Siegel, elected Director of the Company in February&nbsp;2002, is one of the
individuals.&#160; The Company purchased
approximately $606,000 and $448,500 of materials from D-Mac in 2004 and 2003,
respectively.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In 2003, Arthur J. Berry, a
Director of the Company became principal shareholder, owning 80 percent, of Kit
Homebuilders West, L.L.C., a producer of HUD Code manufactured homes.&#160; Kit Homebuilders West, L.L.C. purchased
approximately $452,000 and $216,000 of products from the Company at competitive
market prices during 2004 and 2003, respectively.&#160; Subsequently, in October&nbsp;2004, Arthur J.
Berry sold the majority of his interest in Kit Homebuilders West, L.L.C. to a
non-related third party.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

  <td width="100%" valign="top" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:100.0%;">
  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">NOTE L -
  EMPLOYEE BENEFITS</font></b></h3>
  <p style="line-height:50%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:50%;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>

  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;line-height:20%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:20%;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company has a 401(k)
plan through which the employer matches 50 percent of employees&#146; contributions
up to six percent of wages.&#160; Employees
are eligible for participation in the 401(k) plan after completing one year of
service.&#160; Employer contributions to the
plan were $30,743, $33,371, and $40,022 in 2004, 2003, and 2002, respectively.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">29</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:11.0pt;">

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company had accrued
approximately $30,000 for incurred but not reported items in 2003 and 2002
associated with self-insured employee medical benefits.&#160; As of October&nbsp;1, 2003, the Company was
no longer self-insured for employee medical benefits. The Company&#146;s employees
are now&#160; insured through a traditional
medical care plan.&#160; The Company will
continue to self-insure for employee dental benefits with no plans to accrue
for incurred but not reported items as these amounts are insignificant.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

  <p style="line-height:50%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:50%;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>

  <td width="100%" valign="top" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 3.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:100.0%;">
  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;line-height:20%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:20%;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>
  </td>
 </tr>
</table>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company operates in two business segments:&#160; Axles and Tire Reconditioning and Accessories
and Siding Distribution.&#160; These segments
have been determined by evaluating the Company&#146;s internal reporting structure
and nature of products offered.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Axles and Tire Reconditioning:&#160;
The Company provides reconditioned axles and tires to manufactured
housing factories.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Accessories and Siding Distribution:&#160;
The Company provides skirting, siding, and other aftermarket accessories
to manufactured housing dealers and contractors.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="14%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:14.32%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">12</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.38%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="14%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:14.34%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">73</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.2%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
</table>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company does not assign interest income,
interest expense, other expenses or income taxes to operating segments.&#160; Identifiable assets and related capital
expenditures are assigned to operating locations rather than operating
segments, with depreciation allocated to the segments based upon usage.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">30</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:11.0pt;">

<hr size="2" width="100%" noshade color="gray" align="left">

</font></div>

<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='30',FILE='C:\JMS\jpatrow\04-15333-1\task264925\15333-1-ba-03.htm',USER='jpatrow',CD='Dec 29 14:39 2004' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<table border="1" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" width="100%" style="border:none;border-collapse:collapse;width:100.0%;">
 <tr>
  <td width="100%" valign="top" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:100.0%;">
  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">NOTE N &#150; JOINT VENTURE</font></b></h3>
  <p style="line-height:50%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:50%;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr>
  <td width="100%" valign="top" style="border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 3.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:100.0%;">
  <h3 style="font-weight:bold;line-height:20%;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;line-height:20%;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>
  </td>
 </tr>
</table>

<h3 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:auto;text-autospace:none;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:normal;">&nbsp;</font></b></h3>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company has a 50 percent ownership
interest in a joint venture, NewCo Tire &amp; Axle, L.L.C. (NewCo), which was
formed to engage </font>in
the manufactured housing axle and tire recycling business in Arizona, New
Mexico, Kansas and parts of southern California.&#160; Under the joint venture agreement, the
Company contributed cash and inventory amounting to $389,000 for its initial
investment in 2003.&#160; Further capital
contributions to NewCo are limited to an additional $20,000 in aggregate unless
both parties agree to the additional contributions and are willing to
contribute 50 percent of the required capital.&#160;
The Company has a 40 percent
interest in net profits or losses.&#160;&#160; The
investment in the joint venture is accounted for using the equity method of
accounting, under which the Company&#146;s initial investment is adjusted for its
proportionate share of net profits and losses and distributed earnings, if
any.&#160; NewCo has not made any
distributions of profits during 2003 or 2004 to the joint venture members.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Concurrent with the formation of the joint venture,
the Company leases trucks and equipment to NewCo. Gross rental income
recognized from the NewCo leases was approximately $26,000 and $16,000 in 2004
and 2003, respectively.&#160; The following
information summarizes the activity of the joint venture:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-autospace:none;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

  <td width="63%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:63.5%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-autospace:none;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Equity in
  net assets</font></p>

  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.52%;">
